channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Acclaimed 20th century philosopher Jürgen Habermas dies at 96
item.description: Associated with the Frankfurt School, Habermas was a world-renowned thinker on modernity and democracy who helped shape German post-war and post-reunification political discourse.
item.content:encoded:
Associated with the Frankfurt School, Habermas was a world-renowned thinker on modernity and democracy who helped shape German post-war and post-reunification political discourse.

(Image credit: Louisa Gouliamaki)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Why the Chicago Bears could be moving to Indiana
item.description: While Illinois is trying to keep the team in Chicago's suburbs, Indiana lawmakers are offering a plan to finance a new stadium
item.content:encoded:
While Illinois is trying to keep the team in Chicago's suburbs, Indiana lawmakers are offering a plan to finance a new stadium

(Image credit: Nam Y. Huh)
item.dc:creator: Lydia Calitri

item.title: Pentagon tightens controls over Stars and Stripes after calling it "woke"
item.description: The new rules for the independent military newspaper are the Defense Department's latest effort to put extraordinary restrictions on journalists covering the agency.
item.content:encoded:
The new rules for the independent military newspaper are the Defense Department's latest effort to put extraordinary restrictions on journalists covering the agency.

(Image credit: AMEL EMRIC)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: 'War of the Worlds' remake sinks to the bottom at this year's Razzie Awards
item.description: The surveillance industry version of HG Wells' 1898 classic sci-fi novel stars Ice Cube, and won accolades for worst picture, actor, director and more.
item.content:encoded:
The surveillance industry version of HG Wells' 1898 classic sci-fi novel stars Ice Cube, and won accolades for worst picture, actor, director and more.

(Image credit: Amazon Prime Video)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Russian strike on Kyiv region kills 4 and wounds 15, with peace talks stalled
item.description: The strikes comes after the United States paused ceasefire talks between Russia and Ukraine due to the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded:
The strikes comes after the United States paused ceasefire talks between Russia and Ukraine due to the war with Iran.

(Image credit: Efrem Lukatsky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: In South Carolina, measles shows how far apart neighbors can be on vaccines
item.description: In South Carolina, some parents embrace vaccines, others opt out. Why do people make such different choices? A mix of politics, distrust and misinformation is pushing neighbors apart.
item.content:encoded:
In South Carolina, some parents embrace vaccines, others opt out. Why do people make such different choices? A mix of politics, distrust and misinformation is pushing neighbors apart.

(Image credit: Mike Belleme for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: Brazil's ex-President Bolsonaro is in intensive care with pneumonia, hospital says
item.description: One of Bolsonaro's doctors described the former Brazilian president's medical condition as "serious."
item.content:encoded:
One of Bolsonaro's doctors described the former Brazilian president's medical condition as "serious."

(Image credit: Eraldo Peres)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Opinion: An ancient, sophisticated palate
item.description: Researchers looking at foodcrusts on the pottery shards of ancient humans say there's evidence of a wide variety of ingredients, indicating that they may have been experimenting with "recipes."
item.content:encoded:
Researchers looking at foodcrusts on the pottery shards of ancient humans say there's evidence of a wide variety of ingredients, indicating that they may have been experimenting with "recipes."

(Image credit: Martin Bernetti)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Why women have an especially tough time in Senegal's prisons
item.description: Women charged with a crime in Senegal are at the mercy of a slow judicial process and prisons that may lack basic supplies. They also face stigma that robs them of familial and community support.
item.content:encoded:
Women charged with a crime in Senegal are at the mercy of a slow judicial process and prisons that may lack basic supplies. They also face stigma that robs them of familial and community support.

(Image credit: Ricci Shryock for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ricci Shryock

item.title: The Oscars are Sunday night. Here's how to watch
item.description: The Oscars start at 7 p.m. Eastern, 4 p.m. Pacific on Sunday, airing on ABC and streaming on Hulu.
item.content:encoded:
The Oscars start at 7 p.m. Eastern, 4 p.m. Pacific on Sunday, airing on ABC and streaming on Hulu.

(Image credit: Angela Weiss)
item.dc:creator: Ivy Buck
